extraction.one_line_summary: DEMARE Arnaud won stage 1 on a steep climb topping 1km from finish, only rider able to match Alaphilippe's acceleration before out-sprinting him.
tactical_patterns: FDJ matched traditional crosswind specialists Quickstep/Lotto in echelon positioning despite lacking northern racing pedigree, protecting Demare unexpectedly well.
Stage 1 summit finish with 1km remaining favored puncheur-sprinters over pure sprinters, Demare's dual capacity decisive against separated field.
Crosswinds on opening stages eliminated GC contenders before mountains, reversing traditional Paris-Nice selection pattern where TT/climbs decide.

rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: positive: Only sprinter matching Alaphilippe's acceleration on steep 1km-to-go climb reveals unexpected climbing capacity under race conditions.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Six seconds from yellow after stage 1 near-win puts him ahead of traditional GC men, suggesting stronger overall threat than anticipated.
rider_signals.PORT Richie: negative: Lost 15+ minutes stage 2 after Tour Down Under win and solid training block indicates poor weather adaptation or underlying form issues.
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: Complete non-factor in sprints despite premium field suggests equipment change or tactical misalignment with Giant-Alpecin program.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Yo-yoing off back group in opening two hours stage 2 required full team rescue, revealing vulnerability in crosswind positioning.
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: neutral: Finished with Contador group after splits but showed no proactive strength in crosswind selection.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Hung with reduced front group stage 1 despite not being crosswind specialist, better echelon skills than climbing palmares suggests.
rider_signals.CICCONE Davide: positive: FDJ signing from Lampre consistently at front in crosswinds protecting Demare, immediate tactical integration into French squad culture.
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Jon: neutral: Went off-road at worst moment in stage 1 crosswinds but regrouped to Contador group, limiting damage through positioning recovery.
